findings.evidence: New Wave and Post Punk appear as sibling genres under the same parent. Historically and musically these styles share a large overlap of artists, production aesthetics, and era (late 1970s–1980s), and many playlists would contain both without clear separation.
findings.recommendation: Review whether both nodes provide sufficiently distinct playlist identities within this branch, or whether clearer structural separation or contextualization under a different structural grouping would improve musical distinction.

findings.evidence: The node aggregates Norteño, Texano, Banda, and Ranchero, which are distinct traditions with different instrumentation and rhythmic structures. If content were placed directly at the structural node rather than within its children, playlists could mix highly different styles.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the structural node functions strictly as an organizational container and that musical content is assigned to its specific child genres to maintain playlist cohesion.

findings.node_path: Music > Rock > Alternative & Indie > Post Punk > Coldwave / Darkwave
findings.evidence: The node label combines two historically related but distinct scenes (Coldwave and Darkwave). These styles often overlap but are not identical and may function as separate genre labels in music classification systems.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate whether combining both labels into a single node introduces semantic redundancy or ambiguity in the taxonomy. Consider clarifying the intended scope of the node.

findings.node_path: Music > Rock > Alternative & Indie > Grunge > Seattle Grunge (Atomic)
findings.evidence: Seattle Grunge represents a historically specific scene tied to a particular geographic and cultural context. Further subdivision would likely be arbitrary and reduce musical coherence.
findings.recommendation: Maintain this node as atomic unless a clearly recognized musical sub-style emerges that is widely accepted in musicological or industry classification.

findings.node_path: Music > Rock > Alternative & Indie > Dream Pop (Atomic)
findings.evidence: Dream Pop is already a well-established stylistic category defined by production aesthetics and atmospheric sound. Subdividing further would likely create artificial microgenres without clear musical boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Keep the node atomic unless a widely recognized and musically coherent subgenre appears.

findings.node_path: Music > Pop > Synth & Electronic Pop > Electro Pop (Atomic)
findings.evidence: Electro Pop functions as a recognizable genre boundary with consistent sonic identity centered on electronic production and pop song structures. Additional subdivision would likely reduce classification clarity.
findings.recommendation: Retain atomic status unless the taxonomy later incorporates major distinct electro-pop-derived substyles.
